notes: agents[] is empty — this is an L2 recipe. All domain-work stages are owned by chained L1 recipes (commit-code, create-pr, merge-pr), not domain agents directly. Owner values reflect recipe names.
Stage 2 contains two sequential L1 tasks (commit-code then create-pr) — create-pr depends on commit-code because the branch must have committed work before a PR can be opened.
Scenario validation (Stage 6) covers both ship-level scenarios (S1: developer ready for next feature, S2: reviewer can trace PR to issue) by composing evidence from all three L1 chains.

tasks.subject: Workflow Pre-flight
tasks.description: Infrastructure checks before chaining any L1 recipe. Resolve stm_base from core/config.yaml (stm.base-path). Enforce C1: current branch must not be main, master, or the repository default branch — hard halt if violated. Verify changed files exist (staged, unstaged, or untracked) — graceful exit if none. Verify platform config is present and valid. Hold resolved stm_base for all downstream contract paths.

tasks.subject: Intent Resolution — load ship DAG
tasks.description: Check cache at .meridian/cache/intent-resolution/ship.json. Invalidate if hash(intent.yaml) has changed. If cache is valid, load DAG and skip re-resolution. If stale or absent, invoke intent-resolver with intent_path, workflow_path, config_path, and chained_recipes list to produce this DAG. Write resolved DAG to .meridian/project/issues/{issue}/dag/ship.json. Verify dag.stm_base matches the value resolved in Stage 0.

tasks.subject: L1 Chain: commit-code
tasks.description: Invoke the commit-code L1 recipe as a chained sub-execution. Pass resolved stm_base and issue context. commit-code will: analyze all uncommitted changes, group by concern, map to open issues, produce conventional commits with issue references. Behavioral constraints from ship intent apply: all operations must auto-proceed without human approval gates (C2). commit-code's own failure conditions (F4: wrong issue reference, F6: uncommitted work remains at merge time) are evaluated by commit-code's internal Stage 6. Ship recipe waits for commit-code to reach completed status before proceeding. On failure: hard stop — do not proceed to create-pr.

tasks.subject: L1 Chain: create-pr
tasks.description: Invoke the create-pr L1 recipe as a chained sub-execution after commit-code completes. Pass resolved stm_base and issue context. create-pr will: verify branch is pushed to remote, analyze PR readiness, resolve issue linkage, build quality checklist backed by evidence, create the PR on the configured platform (GitHub per config). Behavioral constraints: auto-proceed without human approval gates (C2). Failure conditions from ship intent: F5 (PR must have linked issue and correct target branch). create-pr's internal Stage 6 validates its own scenarios. Ship recipe waits for create-pr to reach completed status before proceeding to generation.

tasks.subject: L1 Chain: merge-pr
tasks.description: Invoke the merge-pr L1 recipe as a chained sub-execution after create-pr completes. Pass resolved stm_base and issue context. merge-pr will: verify PR is mergeable with no conflicts (C6: conflicts are a hard stop), merge using repository default strategy (C3: no strategy override), switch local checkout to main, pull latest from remote (C4: main must be up to date before merge), delete feature branch locally and remotely (C5). Behavioral constraints: all operations auto-proceed (C2). Ship failure conditions evaluated: F1 (feature branch must not exist after completion), F2 (no merge conflicts), F3 (local on main, main up to date with remote). merge-pr's internal Stage 6 validates its own scenarios. Ship recipe waits for merge-pr to reach completed status.

tasks.subject: Scenario Validation: S1 — Developer ready for next feature
tasks.description: Persona: Developer. Given: the main branch after ship completes. Evaluate: (1) all feature work is reachable from main's history — verify via git log that the feature branch commits appear on main; (2) feature branch is absent locally and remotely — verify no local ref and no remote ref for the branch; (3) main is current with remote — verify local HEAD matches remote HEAD. Pass criteria: all three checks pass. Failure condition F1 (branch still exists) and F3 (not on main or main not up to date) must both be clear. If any check fails, mark scenario failed and block evidence stage.

tasks.subject: Scenario Validation: S2 — Code reviewer can trace shipped work
tasks.description: Persona: Code Reviewer. Given: the merged pull request on the git platform. Evaluate: (1) PR is merged and visible on platform with correct base branch (main); (2) PR body contains a linked issue reference traceable to the originating issue — failure condition F5 (no linked issue or wrong branch) must be clear; (3) PR body contains quality checklist and evidence from create-pr; (4) merge commit is present on main's history. Pass criteria: reviewer can confirm the merge landed on main, trace work to its issue, and review evidence without external context. If any check fails, mark scenario failed and block evidence stage.

tasks.subject: Evidence and Close
tasks.description: Collect and write evidence artifacts to STM. Write ship-level evidence file to {stm_base}/{issue}/evidence/ship/{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.md — include: scenario validation results (S1, S2 pass/fail), final git state (current branch, HEAD SHA, feature branch absent confirmation), PR URL and merge status, references to L1 evidence files produced by commit-code, create-pr, and merge-pr. Write final DAG state (all tasks marked completed) to {stm_base}/{issue}/dag/ship.json. Update cache at .meridian/cache/intent-resolution/ship.json with current intent_hash. Recipe is closed.
